title: Define the canonical resumable work transaction model
goal: Translate the plan-defined transaction meaning, lifecycle semantics, active-transaction invariants, and identity roles into implementation-facing documentation and executable review criteria without adding requirements beyond plan.md.
scope: Capture the canonical meaning of a transaction exactly as specified in plan.md.
Capture the distinction between ticket_id, tx_id, seq, and session_id exactly as specified in plan.md.
Capture the single-active-transaction rule, begin-versus-resume rule, commit-non-terminal rule, and explicit terminal completion rule exactly as specified in plan.md.
Produce implementation-facing acceptance checks that can later be used against runtime code and tests.
out_of_scope: Adding requirements not present in plan.md.
Redefining persistence schema details that belong to p1-t03.
Redefining state-machine continuation rules that belong to p1-t02.
expected_outputs: A transaction-model specification derived strictly from the cited plan sections.
A traceability table linking each requirement in scope to exact plan sections and exact implementation review targets.
A gap checklist for current code that distinguishes plan requirements from current observations.
Implementation-facing guidance for how ops_* helper contracts must preserve the canonical boundary between server-owned runtime identity and client-managed helper inputs.
A completed ticket artifact at docs/v0.6.0/p1-t01-spec.md whose source usage is consistent with the ticket inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Every requirement in scope is traceable to explicit plan.md sections listed in source_plan_sections.
The ticket artifact does not add canonical requirements that are absent from plan.md.
The ticket artifact clearly separates plan requirements from current-code observations and future implementation work.
The output is detailed enough that later implementation tickets can validate runtime behavior without inventing new semantics.

When ops_* helper contracts are updated in downstream implementation work, they reference docs/v0.6.0/ops-tool-contract-alignment-plan.md for identity-boundary alignment without promoting task_id or session_id into canonical runtime identity.
The completed p1-t01 artifact explicitly checks that the ticket inputs are complete, non-contradictory, and sufficient for the ticket scope.

REQ-P1-IDENTITY: Server-owned transaction variables vs client-managed inputs

forbidden_moves: Do not infer additional transaction statuses beyond plan.md.
Do not promote task_id or session_id into canonical runtime identity.
Do not redefine transaction success to include verify or commit success alone.
